title: Misato and Shinji in Episode 23
wikitext:
[]: Misato & Shinji in Episode 23'             Quote Post by Silent Lightning » Wed Oct 08, 2008 3:31 pm In the Big Irony Bomb scene, what evidence is there actually to suggest that Misato is "putting the moves" on Shinji like she once said? Am I missing something here? Somebody educate me on this, please...

[]: Re: Misato & Shinji in Episode 23'             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Oct 08, 2008 4:09 pm Silent Lightning wrote: In the Big Irony Bomb scene, what evidence is there actually to suggest that Misato is "putting the moves" on Shinji like she once said? Am I missing something here? Somebody educate me on this, please...

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Wed Oct 08, 2008 4:30 pm And you can't forget the 'umbrella' as phallic symbol when Misato sits down next to Shinji. I don't have the pictures, will someone else provide it for future reference?

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Oct 08, 2008 5:14 pm I always that it was a "motherly" thing. Like when a son or daughter is upset and his/her mother comes in to check up on him/her and sits next to him/her on the bed; like that. I personally don't think she was trying to put the moves on him, just went in to check up on him.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Oct 08, 2008 5:18 pm Action_Bastard wrote: I always that it was a "motherly" thing.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Oct 08, 2008 6:36 pm Shinji's reaction, "Misato! No!" tells us what he thinks she was up to, as does his comment in ep. 24 to Kaworu that he doesn't want to go home. In EOE he tells the comatose Asuka that he's now afraid of Rei and Misato. Well, we know why he's afraid of Rei, but I wonder why he's afraid of Misato........ Also, in ep. 25 they flash back to the ep. 23 scene in question during the sequence where the other characters are telling her what a dirty slut she is. What more evidence do you need than that?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Oct 08, 2008 6:43 pm Don't forget that in the DC version of the scene compared to the On air version, when Misato sits down, Shinji's chair in the background looks like a phallic symbol. (There was a topic on this; I can't remember the title at the moment.) Personally, I see the bit in episode 23 explains not only Misato's actions in EoE but also EoTV (when we get the flashback TO 23).

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Oct 08, 2008 7:27 pm CorporalChaos wrote: And you can't forget the 'umbrella' as phallic symbol when Misato sits down next to Shinji. I don't have the pictures, will someone else provide it for future reference?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Oct 08, 2008 7:50 pm I think there definately is a sexual undertone to Misato's actions but it's not something she's consciously expressing. It's just that she only knows how to deal with men in any form if it somehow relates to sex and lesser forms of physical affection which she mistakenly conflates with love. Shinji's is rather extreme, certainly, but I don't think he's bluntly aware of what's in the back of her mind here. Rather it's probably a combination of just wanting to be left alone and feeling intense shame at his own inability to comfort her in ep 21 under similar circumstances. His fear of Misato likely stems from her 'the right to survive' rant she gives him after he kills Kaworu as well as just generally not feeling like the apartment is a home anymore with all the shit that has gone down. On a side note, why in the world didn't Misato exploit Kaworu's death to get Shinji motivated in EOE? I believe something like "If you're just going to give up now then you killed Kaworu for nothing!" would cut right through his funk and force him to at least acknowledge her presence.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Wed Oct 08, 2008 11:15 pm I agree pretty much 100%, although apparently the Newtype film book says something about Misato offering Shinji her body, IIRC. Could somebody supply the relevant passage, maybe?

[]:             Quote Post by Zerogoki » Thu Oct 09, 2008 5:04 pm SSD wrote: Don't forget that in the DC version of the scene compared to the On air version, when Misato sits down, Shinji's chair in the background looks like a phallic symbol. (There was a topic on this; I can't remember the title at the moment.)

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Oct 09, 2008 7:18 pm frankly, I don't see it

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Fri Oct 10, 2008 1:20 pm Neither do I. The DC version in fact looks considerably less phallic than the original Ep23.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Oct 10, 2008 1:30 pm I blocked all these Shinji: Stop it, Misato! Misato: Sometimes, I lose all hope for myself! I hate it! IBUKI: Filth, you're so dirty. RITSUKO: You're pathetic. Asuka: Disgusting! You're just foul! That's what you call a mature relationship?! You make me puke!

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Oct 10, 2008 1:53 pm AyrYntake wrote: Neither do I. The DC version in fact looks considerably less phallic than the original Ep23.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Fri Oct 10, 2008 2:09 pm I believe it was Freud what said: Sometimes a Cigar is just a Cigar

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Fri Oct 10, 2008 2:40 pm Freud said that? That goes against everything Freud said! To remain on topic, Misato was trying to pull the moves on Shinji because it's emotionally confusing for everyone involved (including those watching) and Anno wanted to get that feeling across to the audience. I don't see why everyone always tries to explain this scene in the context of the episode without taking into account Anno himself. Also, this "Misato x Shinji?" topic races my total number of dimes to almost enough to buy a snack.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Fri Oct 10, 2008 3:10 pm Somebody said it.... I checked it anyway, and it's listed as unsourced on Wikiquote.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Oct 10, 2008 3:28 pm I think Jung was the one who made the comment about the cigar. Freud got all pissed about it at the very least, I remember.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Oct 10, 2008 8:18 pm The Bastard King wrote: I don't see why everyone always tries to explain this scene in the context of the episode without taking into account Anno himself.
